entries.context: Very common insult in Russia. Heard in road rage and disputes.
entries.suggested_response: Say 'Izvinite' (Excuse me / Sorry) and walk away.

entries.context: Classic Russian insult calling someone a fool. Very common and not considered extremely offensive.
entries.suggested_response: Ignore or shrug it off. Not worth responding to.

entries.context: Used to call someone a rogue or bad person. More serious than basic insults.
entries.suggested_response: Stay calm. Do not mirror the aggression.

entries.context: Very offensive — implies the person is human filth. High hostility signal.
Accusation of theft. Note: 'Vor v zakone' (thief in law) is also a criminal title in Russia.
entries.suggested_response: Show your belongings. Say 'Ya ne vor' (I am not a thief).

entries.context: Insult targeting appearance or behavior. Common in confrontations.
entries.suggested_response: Do not respond to personal attacks. Exit the situation calmly.
